free_text: Excitation function of HE3 induced nuclear reactions
on natPt up to 26MeV
High purity (99.9%) Pt foils with 10 micro-m thickness
Three stacks were
irradiated with HE3 particles, in each stack the Pt
foils were together with Ti (10 micro-m) monitor foils.
Two stacks were irradiated at 23 MeV primary beam
energy for 1 h with 150 nA beam intensity, the third
stack was irradiated at 27 MeV energy for 1 h with
200 nA beam intensity. The energy on the first foil
was estimated by using the calibrated settings of the
cyclotron. The decay and spectrometric characteristics
of the investigated radionuclides were taken from the
NUDAT 2.5 database.
Chapter 4
The energy uncertainty of the primary beam
was +- 0.3MeV. The uncertainty of the energy of the
bombarding beam in the middle of each irradiated foil
was estimated combining the uncertainties of the
energy degradation depending on the thickness of the
target foils in the stack and the energy straggling.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the last foil
was +-2.5 MeV.
The overall uncertainty amounted to 11-17%.
detector efficiency (5%),
beam current(8%),
number of target nuclei(5%)
nuclear data (1-5%);
statistical and peak fitting (1-8%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EMPIRE,
and ALICE.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
